rubric: Explains that identical author and committer metadata is rendered as one combined row.
Identifies the equality contract as matching name, email, and date.
Preserves the separate Author and Committer rows when any identity field differs.
Keeps the tiny UI change compact and does not invent tests, behavior, or risk absent from the diff.
Explains the end-to-end walkthrough progress flow from agent events through Electron IPC to renderer state.
Covers the backend-specific streaming implementations for Codex, Claude Code, and OpenCode, including fallback behavior where material.
Identifies stale-generation protection and the semantic analyzing-versus-response progress phases.
Prioritizes the runtime behavior and its regression tests over styling and repetitive fixture changes.
Explains how the shared walkthrough surface becomes an interactive merge-request review experience.
Covers general comments, inline review comments, editing, submission, navigation, and review actions without flattening them into a file list.
Connects the shared app changes to extracted reusable diff, markdown, preference, and review helpers.
Distinguishes core behavior and tests from CSS, dependency, lockfile, and build-configuration support changes.
